item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: April inflation data is widely expected to show it has risen to a nearly 3-year high when the Consumer Price Index is released Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: Discount Grocery Stores Thrive As Variety Of Economic Pressures Weigh On Consumers
media:content.media:description: Inflation jumped 0.9% from February to March, the largest month-to-month jump since 2022.

item.title: Inflation expected to jump to a 3-year high as Iran war continues to fuel rising prices

item.description: President Donald Trump told reporters the Iranian response to a U.S. peace proposal was “stupid” and that the ceasefire is “on massive life support.” NBC News' Jon Allen reports on the war's impasse and how Trump responded to questions over his meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping later in the week.

media:content.media:title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'on massive life support'
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump told reporters the Iranian response to a U.S. peace proposal was “stupid” and that the ceasefire is “on massive life support.” NBC News' Jon Allen reports on the war's impasse and how Trump responded to questions over his meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping later in the week.

item.title: Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'on massive life support'

item.dc:creator: Joe Murphy
Jiachuan Wu
item.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

media:content.media:text: This U.S. state map shows how much in state, federal taxes and fees are tacked on to a gallon of gas. Values range from 18 cents to more than 60 cents per gallon.
This U.S. state map shows how much in state, federal taxes and fees are tacked on to a gallon of gas. Values range from 18 cents to more than 60 cents per gallon.
This U.S. state map shows how much in state, federal taxes and fees are tacked on to a gallon of gas. Values range from 18 cents to more than 60 cents per gallon.

item.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes

item.dc:creator: Megan Lebowitz
item.description: Suspending the federal gas tax would require congressional approval, and it could cost the government billions of dollars in revenue.

media:content.media:text: A sign displays fuel prices at a Mobil gas station
media:content.media:description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said Sunday that President Donald Trump was “open to all ideas” to cut the cost of gas.

item.title: Trump says he would support suspending the federal gas tax

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: LONDON — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer dug his heels in Tuesday and defied widespread calls to resign

media:content.media:text: Labour's Local Election Campaign Enters Final Days
media:content.media:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in London on May 5

media:content.media:text: Sir Keir Starmer speech - London
media:content.media:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer in London on May 5

item.title: Oust me if you can: Britain’s Starmer challenges mutinous party

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
Bridget Bowman
Ben Kamisar
item.description: Tuesday’s primary elections in Nebraska will set the stage for November in a battleground House district, as well as a potentially competitive Senate contest, as Democrats try to win control of Congress.

media:content.media:text: “There’s some things I wish were better,” Bacon said. “But am I going to vote to raise taxes on middle-class Americans? I’m not.”
media:content.media:description: Retiring Republican Rep. Don Bacon's Nebraska district will be a key battleground for both parties in the fight for the House majority.

media:content.media:text: Nebraska Democratic State Senator John Cavanaugh
media:content.media:description: Retiring Republican Rep. Don Bacon's Nebraska district will be a key battleground for both parties in the fight for the House majority.

media:content.media:text: Denise Powell stands inside of a room smiling with her arms cross
media:content.media:description: Retiring Republican Rep. Don Bacon's Nebraska district will be a key battleground for both parties in the fight for the House majority.

media:content.media:text: Cindy Burbank and William Forbes are facing off in Nebraska's Democratic Senate primary.
media:content.media:description: Retiring Republican Rep. Don Bacon's Nebraska district will be a key battleground for both parties in the fight for the House majority.

media:content.media:text: Sen. Shelley Moore Capito, R-W.V., speaks during a news conference
media:content.media:description: Retiring Republican Rep. Don Bacon's Nebraska district will be a key battleground for both parties in the fight for the House majority.

item.title: Tuesday’s primaries to set up key fall matchups in Nebraska

item.dc:creator: Tyler Kingkade
item.description: A growing number of families and educators say they’re fed up with i-Ready’s personalized math and reading lessons, which feature repetitive cartoons and slow voice-overs.

media:content.media:text: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math session on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, CA, on Monday, May 11, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: Ward shows how an i-Ready game works on his school-issued Chromebook.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: Parents cheer the passing of a resolution to limit screen time at a recent Los Angeles school board meeting.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: Ward and his friends' frequent complaints about i-Ready prompted his father to research the program.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: A bald man with black eyeglasses rests his chin on one hand while seated at a desk in a bedroom.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: A goateed man wearing a checkered shirt standing for a portrait in the shade.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: Curriculum Associates CEO Rob Waldron
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: A young man holding an award certificate and smiling broadly.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: A person seen from the waist down holding a hand-written sign reading Save Our Kids.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

media:content.media:text: Bridie Lee's son reads a Garfield comic book during an LAUSD board meeting, waiting for his turn to speak in the public comment session.
media:content.media:description: Ward Wooden, 14, demonstrates a math lesson on i-Ready at his family’s home in Studio City, Calif.

item.title: The revolt against i-Ready: Private equity-backed company faces parent, teacher and student fury

item.description: The 18 Americans evacuated from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived at medical facilities in Omaha and Atlanta. One passenger who tested mildly positive and another showing symptoms were placed in isolation. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports.

media:content.media:title: Inside American passengers’ journey home from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
media:content.media:description: The 18 Americans evacuated from the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrived at medical facilities in Omaha and Atlanta. One passenger who tested mildly positive and another showing symptoms were placed in isolation. NBC News’ Camila Bernal reports.

item.title: Inside American passengers’ journey home from hantavirus-hit cruise ship

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: Meta, the parent of Facebook and Instagram, is failing to stop scammers who repeatedly buy ads on the platforms to target seniors with scams, a new report says.

media:content.media:text: In scam ads viewed by the researchers, viewers were promised free money via Medicare.
media:content.media:description: In scam ads viewed by the researchers, viewers were promised free money via Medicare.

item.title: Fake Trump and Oprah ads fuel a wave of Medicare scams on Facebook, report says

item.description: Americans from the hantavirus cruise ship are being assessed and monitored at the National Quarantine Unit in Nebraska. NBC News' Camila Bernal reports on where the passengers are staying.

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts
